sources_audited.note: The cited Census URL opens the general NAICS search page with a sector-44 query and does not surface code 445320 or the claimed six-digit definition.
The BLS May 2023 NAICS 445300 table reports 173,760 jobs and the cited cashier, salesperson, supervisor, and stocker shares; it also states that estimates exclude self-employed workers.
Accenture reports the cited 50% potentially impacted retail hours, 93% three-to-five-year AI investment intention, and retail customer, inventory, and associate-assistance use cases.
Gallup reports that 22% of employer-business owners planned to sell or transfer within five years versus 9% of nonemployers, from its fall 2024 survey.

McKinsey reports that more than half of small-business owners are over 55, estimates six million ownership exits over the next decade, and gives the cited 2022 split of 92% closures, 5% sales, and 3% transfers.
The BizBuySell PDF contains numerous 2021-2024 completed NAICS 445310 liquor-store comparables, including 2024 annual sales from about $1.1 million to $10.74 million.
The BLS article reports that packaged alcohol represented 90% of NAICS 4453 sales in 2017, describes the products as relatively uniform, and shows margin output moving with sales output.
IWSR reports the cited preliminary 2025 total-market volume declines by category and that cost was cited by 31% of US drinkers moderating consumption.
WSWA reports 2024 wine and spirits volume and revenue declines, moderation and abstinence trends, premixed-cocktail growth, and rapid convenience-channel expansion.
The report states that specialized beer, wine, and liquor store sales reached $73.8 billion in 2024, up 2.4%, and describes competition from grocery, warehouse, and convenience channels.

caveats: S1 is reachable but the cited query does not display the claimed NAICS 445320 definition; this weakens the definitional support used for e and o but cannot change the null base tier or EVIDENCE_FIRST action caused by missing n.

The a and rho values extrapolate from retail-wide affected-hours and CxO investment-intention claims to labor substitution and realized implementation in independent liquor stores. The packet labels both inputs PROXY and states the likely upward bias, but the numeric mapping remains judgment-heavy.

The e and s5 ranges are not measured for the lens population: broker comparables establish that stores can transfer, while Gallup and McKinsey use broad all-industry ownership populations and different event denominators. The packet discloses these limitations.

The q value has no direct evidence on five-year retention of AI benefits. S7 supports product uniformity and S9 supports channel pressure, but neither measures pass-through or operator retention, so the ESTIMATE label is essential.

The d5 bridge converts one-year, all-channel national volume evidence and specialist-store dollar sales into a five-year constant-quality quantity range. This is a broad but disclosed proxy, and the 0.75-1.02 range appropriately carries substantial uncertainty.

The o estimate is directionally plausible because alcohol requires licensed physical fulfillment, but its level is not directly measured and may conflate continued need for some licensed operator with continued volume through eligible specialist-store operators as competing channels gain share.

S10 is a secondary compiled report that relays figures attributed to NIQ and BW166 rather than exposing the underlying datasets or full methods; its dollar-sales evidence should not be read as direct quantity evidence.
summary: Mechanics pass and all ten primitive-cited sources were reopened. Most cited factual claims are supported, and the record honestly labels broad extrapolations and judgment as PROXY or ESTIMATE. The Census definition URL is unsupported as cited, while transferability, retention, five-year demand, and operator-share inputs remain weakly measured. These defects are caveats rather than material findings under the frozen test because n is already MISSING, leaving no base tier and forcing EVIDENCE_FIRST regardless of corrections to the researched inputs.
